Followup email from Tarolyn: approved at First Independence Bank for a $75,000 Line of credit at 3.9%, CD secured by personal money from Tarolyn.

• Met at WCC Business expo- discussed his struggles there with flatline in revenue.
  • Did MKTG analysis (screenshot below)

  • Avg job is 1800 and 3600 in margin, needs to build pipeline and get website/marketing going.

ClientIntake.CompanyName: Maple Ridge Graphics LLC - TBD Start

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Lance Wolfe <lwolfe@mtu.edu>

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

Scheduled Facebook posts through the month


He has some jobs coming up, found on the internet and referral - not sure if he can do it due to capacity constraints - his drone won't hold enough. Needs 250 lbs, can only do 50 lbs at a time.


Rockingpicking service maps are being created.


Blakes is not resonding



RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

Reach out to Blakes and let them know that he is filling up to create urgency


Learn google ads - do minimum amount while learning - sent Febrary 6


16 clients for the season



NEXT STEPS & FOLLOW-UP ACTIONS:


He is going to spend time learning google ads and filing his calendar for the spring. Meeting in 2 weeks to review questions on Google Ads




IMPACT REMINDER: Discussed during initial

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

Emails with Alison, they could not come to an agreement on purchase price and decided not to move forward.

Client and I reviewed client's first quarter P/L. Client's gross revenues were up by 48% reflecting $273k in 1st quarter revenues but his net profit was down. We went line by line, subtracted the expenses incurred by client that were more for tax purposes and zeroed in on the labor increase. Client felt this was due primarily to the initial inefficiencies of the new contract with Bronson Athletic Club. We expect that labor as a % should correct in the 2nd quarter. Client submitted his quote to Bronson Commons at $1.20/lb

Met with clients and we reviewed item by item the prices set for last year and which prices to raise. I pushed client to shrink his menu a little-get rid of the products requiring a lot of prep and finishing. We also looked at the impact of the new esta rules and how that will impact client's bottom line

Re: Exclusivity


Daniel Yoder <dayoder@mtu.edu>

8:58 AM (0 minutes ago)

to Heather


Heather,


If you can't offer exclusivity based on sales or guaranteed minimum orders, then what other ways could we frame the conversation that don't relate to sales figures?


Here are different ways of talking about exclusivity. Which sounds better?


Option 1 - "If I only had my products in a couple stores, I would not be able to sell enough to have a viable business. I believe that I need to be in almost every little store to hope to make this business profitable."


Option 2 - "Exclusive territory - I offer my retail partners an exclusive territory of a 1 mile radius from their location, reviewed annually."


Option 3 - "Exclusivity arrangement - I offer my retail partners an exclusive arrangement of only 1 establishment per primary establ

Several new clients came on board -


1099 contractors - very small - one person is to help with book keeping, administraive work, testing websites.

second person to help with client work, she is a student at delta / northwood. Coming on to schedule posts - shadow for content sessions and making graphics.


2 clients are full time clients.


10 clients currently. 6 regular monthly retainer clients adding 1 more. 1-2 additional projects that are going on.


$1000 a month


January 2155 - income - net income 1850

February 2000 income - 1600 expenses

March 1150 income - 860 net


Raised rates at the beginning of the year.


Double / Triple where we are at. Check back in 3 months.
Sam Augusta
Sam Augusta <saugusta@michigansbdc.org>

Client has completed formation of Alternative Acquisitions with his mentor. They have identified two small stamping businesses for sale and have settled into negotiations with St. Charles stamping. We discussed the financing and the sales approach to grow the revenues if the purchase is completed

LLC


Client is looking to do a flower business and needs an LLC to operate under. She was originally looking to open a dog kennel business and also make dog treats. Currently she does not have an LLC for either business.


Re: SBDC 30 Day Follow-UpExternal


Inbox

Search for all messages with label Inbox

Remove label Inbox from this conversation

D; Client wants to start brick and mortar edible arrangements with chocolate. Review BP


ACP: Startup


R/A:

  1. Need full BP/FIN
  2. Looking at $10-20k for equipment. 5C: 580. Client is disabled and lost job = bad credit score.
  3. Recommend starting with just chocolate suckers.
  4. Find out if need commercial kitchen?
  5. Sales Tax
  6. TM/Marketing
  7. IRS Expenses

NS:

  1. Client will review f/u email
  2. Client will send BP

Meeting Resources. Sent email


It was a pleasure meeting with you and discussing your business. Here are the resources I mentioned. 


Business Plan: https://www.score.org/sites/default/files/2023-02/SCORE-Startup-Business-Plan-Template.docx


Cash Flow By Month: https://michigansbdc.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/06/Cash-Flow-Projection-and-Variance-Analysis-Spreadsheet.xlsx


Michigan Sales Tax Information:

FAQ: https://www.michigan.gov/taxes/business-taxes/sales-use-tax/information/general-faq, https://www.michigan.gov/taxes/business-taxes/sales-use-tax

Analysis: He has successfully completed his application for the $5K accelerated grant. He is currently focused on conducting research for his VR project, which is still in the planning stages.

Recommendation: He should continue conducting research to refine his VR project. In parallel, developing a comprehensive business plan will help clarify his objectives, outline the steps needed for success, and strengthen his position for securing future funding.

Next Steps:

  1. Work on a detailed business plan for the VR project, including goals, market research, and financial projections.

Michelle,

My apologies that I'm delayed in sharing David Hornreich's information. He assisted me in developing my website and now he has a site with featured templates that can be used to update your website.


https://www.launchitandloveit.com/

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

Client is building an app to improve wellness outcomes for individuals with a health issue. Specifically, interpret lab results and provide actionable activities to ultimately improve future results. This app will customize recomendations based on the individual through AI. Initial oal is to have businesses offer the app to employees as a part of their HR package.

RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

Need revise the website, make it more user friendly, identify goals and objectives of the app's initial target audience. Need to add a blog or newsletter to gain early adoptors and gage interest. Finanlize team and identify skills to complement the existing members. Appl

• Looked at a property, waiting on realtor to give some of the finals 
  • Registered for an LLC in January
    • Also got EIN number 
    • No bank account yet 
  • Determining next steps today 
  • Talked to a lot of equipment manufactures, and has quotes from two different companies 
  • 3.2 Million
    • Equipment
    • Land

Respond to client about calendar, meetings, check calendar



Tamisha Robinson

Marketing and New Shop Oversight

Prep time includes review of marketing slides Tim sent prior to the meeting


The new Saginaw shop is doing well, exceeding Bay City by 25% daily

This may be a carryover from start up press or may be ongoing, too soon to tell

Is now running a commissary with new expenses related to ice cream transport from Saginaw to Bay CIty

Has implemented food quality testing

He had to renegotiate with the dairy plant manager (higher wage)

Bakery plant manager may end up leaving

Also renegotiated with store managers, who are at $18.50

Tim's priorities are Safety, then Quality, then Marketing

Right now the biggest issue is time spent on tasks from ops to marketing

Marketing is going very well, but takes a lot of time


Advice

  • Over the next 2-4 weeks begin to plot activities that could be delegated

I met with Lisa Hill on April 6 to discuss the competitive analysis for her business. However, during the course of meeting with one of the competitors, Olsen Party Rentals indicated to her that he might be interested in selling his business., which they've owned for 34 years. They offer tables, chairs, canopies, a popcorn machine, linens, everything needed for a party planning business. Lisa owns tables and chairs. She set up a meeting for April 11th to have a sit-down with the owner and discuss the sale in detail. In the meantime, I contacted Dan Brophy for a business valuation form and an attorney who specializes in sales of businesses- John Carter. Lisa will get back to me after the meeting.

Spent time vetting BP and especially financials - they are thorough but not in the correct format.

Spent time on zoom telling Aaron my suggestions.


After all that he told me he's still evaluating between the car tinting and another business - senior dementia care.


So that's what we should have been evaluation and not worrying about final format.


We will talk again in 2 weeks to evaluate both business ideas.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Since this was my first official meeting with Jennifer, I took some time to review her session notes, as well as reviewing the information sent to her by Elizabeth Propp at CMURC. I think there was a little confusion between the Great Lakes Bay Small Business Hub grant and the Right Choice program offered in the modules, as Jennifer said that she "knew all of the information already." She said that during her time in the last year, she:

  • Filed an LLC and registered a DBA under it
  • Got her EIN
  • Opened a Square account to take credit card payments
  • Created a Facebook business page
  • Had a lawyer help her create a release form, releasing her of any liability for future losses or gains that students may have
  • Established pricing: $85 per person for a 2-hour lesson. Includes an info card (cost $1.79 ea.)
  • Run a few test runs of the lesson with family members. They had a good time

Areas where she is struggling includ
Accounting and transaction cleanup for reconciliation, wave payments, invoices, UIA filings. All is looking good, and Brad is doing a great job at keeping up with it. Check in in 90 days or so.

 Hi Dan,

 

Thank you for your support and the valuable assistance from the Michigan SBDC and the SBDC-sponsored Peerspectives CEO Roundtable. As you are aware, I have been participating in the Peerspectives Roundtable since its launch in Livonia in 2006. I am pleased to confirm that the sale of RTI Laboratories, Inc. was completed for $9.15 million, with the real estate selling for $3.6 million. The sale transaction closed on January 31, 2025. CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Meeting Summary:

Laura and Nancy discussed Laura's progress and a new pilot funding opportunity designed to help non-technical founders build initial software prototypes or Minimum Viable Products (MVPs).

  1. New Pilot Program ($5k for MVP): Nancy explained a new SBDC/MEDC pilot program offering up to $5,000 specifically for building an MVP or prototype. This program was partly inspired by challenges faced by founders like Laura. The goal is to get a tangible product into users' hands early for feedback and validation, justifying the software spend to MEDC.
  2. Pilot Program Requirements: To qualify, applicants need:
    • Rigorous Customer Discovery Artifacts: Documented evidence of customer need, including direct quotes, problem descriptions, and urgency scoring from the target market.
    • User Commitment: Commitment from at least ten target user

Tom has been frustrated by not being able to find z suitable building site for his first home project. He has another idea for the business.


He wants to explore buying equipment and running a forestry business cleaning up and mulching fallen trees. He sent a draft of his business plan and asked for my review.


I reviewed the plan and called him to discuss the idea.


It seems to be a legitimate opportunity if he can find financing and equipment in a timely period. We discussed the need to find good, used equipment with low hours to avoid mechanical challenges. I asked about financing and he currently works with Nicolet and will speak with them about loan options.


I let him know that I will be ready to assist with anything and can help with additional financing options if needed.

Anglie has sent multiple emails and texts about the feasibility of AA. We are trying to determine if she should shut down AA, and are considering the variables.


The $11,250 in expenses below, what does that cover.


How many BT’s and BCBA’s work out of AA and what do their earn a year?


We are not looking at shared expenses right now.


Lastly, are you at capacity in Ypsilanti? Isn’t most of the care in the field? Can you take on more clients in that location?

Notes from our call today/

Nick,

My thanks again for your time today. Just a few more details to cover from our conversation.


  1. We talked through some of the lender requirements, sharing that they'll typically expect you to invest between 10% and 20% (closer to 10% under an SBA loan) as a cash down payment to purchase a building
  2. Having additional assets, such as your car or a home, is the type of collateral a lender will also like to see to qualify for a loan 
  3. We discussed and I shared the link to loopnet.com so that you can find current for sale commercial properties in Battle Creek
  4. Your background includes repairing exercise equipment and you have not taught fitness classes. Your strength is in administration and management
  5. Your goal is to open this facility by August 2025 and we'll do what we can to position you to achieve this goal
  6. You already spoke with Matt with the Calhoun Health Dept for the smoo

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Email sent to Jennifer following our meeting:


Hi Jennifer,


Thank you again for meeting with me today! I've included some resources below to help you with the grant:

  • A PDF document on how to add documents to your Google Drive and access them to edit in Google Docs
  • The executive summary template for the grant (this is the largest document needed to apply)
  • The proposal form template
  • The use of funds template

The templates are 3 of the 5 forms needed to apply for the grant. The others are a W-9 (available in the application portal), and a scope of work (essentially a PDF file of screenshots or quotes, verifying the amount asked on your use of funds form). 


Additionally, I wanted to give you a few no-code, free web-builders that might be helpful!

I would recommend checking out:

  • Notion (I even have a template site built,

Re: quick check-in

Analysis:

Jaelyn and her sister have made solid progress by completing both their financial plan and business plan. However, during the review, we discovered a few discrepancies in the numbers. They had used a different financial format that unfortunately did not include critical components like a profit and loss statement or a balance sheet, which are essential for evaluating the viability and sustainability of the project.


They are planning to apply for funding through the Housing Trust Fund, so it’s especially important that their projections are clear, organized, and aligned with the format preferred by grant reviewers.

Recommendation:

They should rework their financials using the provided template. This format is much more accessible, organized, and designed to highlight the most important metrics. It includes everything needed — income statements, profit and loss projections, balance sheets, and detailed brea

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Set appointment


Thank you for your responses. Please use https://calendly.com/southeastmisbdc/phone-call to set a meeting. 


What to Expect

  • We are funded by the Small Business Administration and our services are at no-cost (except some trainings have a small registration fee). 
  • All information we discuss will remain confidential. This information was in the initial documentation that you completed when you requested consulting.
  • Meetings usually last less than 1 hour. Meetings are only by phone and by appointment.
  • We assist both start-up and existing businesses. Some of the topics that we help with are startup steps, business plans, marketing strategies, financial benchmarking, projections and preparing for bank loans.
  • Michigan SBDC does not have any direc

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Met with Asish at his business, which is a gas station, convenience store (liquor license), U-Haul rental location. He owns both the building and the business. He paid cash 2 years ago and currently has no business debt. He is looking for assistance in two areas.

  • First, he is seeking anywhere from $150,000 - $200,000 to do improvements to his current location in South Boardman. Mainly, he wants to install a new tank that would allow him to sell diesel fuel. He also would like to do minor renovations to part of his building so he can open a commercial kitchen and serve food.
  • Second, he is looking at purchasing another gas station/c-store in the area. He does not have a location chosen yet, but one in a similar type location. He expects this to run around $800,000 - $1,200,000.
  • He currently banks with credit union based out of Grand Rapids, but does some business with local Huntington Bank branch in nearby Kalkaska. He is non-committed to either of them, and

Becky has an opportunity to move into a physical location and still keep the food trailer operating. Last year she did about $110,000 in sales on the trailer with approx 12 % profit. She still owes $20,000 from original loan and is current with payments and taxes. She has been operating April throught November each year.

The location she is considering is at 2891 Pontiac Lake Rd., Waterford, MI. It was a Pizza shop that closed in Oct. 2024. She needs to do some research on why and what kind of sales volume she had. This space would not require extensive build out. Initial rent is estimated at $2,000 a month for 1300 sq feet.

I want her to look at other locations for comparision and higher traffic flow. In the mean time start planning to pay off existing loan and or save money to support the new location.

Sending templates.

Disculpa la demora en responder. Estuve fuera de la oficina el viernes y al regresar el martes me encontré con muchos correos pendientes, pero ya me estoy poniendo al día.

Mañana te enviaré un correo con enlaces a recursos importantes para que puedas operar legalmente. También deberíamos agendar una reunión para ponernos al día y revisar juntos las proyecciones. Te recomiendo que agendemos una videollamada para que pueda mostrarte la hoja de cálculo para las proyecciones.

Estaré de viaje en México del 13 al 23 de abril, así que no estaré disponible durante esos días.

Aquí puedes reservar la videollamada:

👉

Meeting Summary:

The meeting covered updates on several clients and procedural discussions, particularly concerning Business Accelerator Fund (BAF) applications.

  1. Fleetwood: An SOW is being processed. Toni will monitor the electronic signing process. Fleetwood is confirmed as a Michigan company eligible for BAF submission, which participants hope will help them succeed in a new market.
  2. Lily Pad / Aurora: This was the main focus. Amanda is handling a complex BAF request via Aurora for Lily Pad, structured in three sequential sections totaling $9700 ($4200 + $4200 + $1300). The current process requires approval of section 1 before submitting sections 2 and 3, which raises concerns about timing and complexity. There was discussion about potential rule changes regarding discretionary funding over the $15k threshold.
    • Proposed Solution: Alain (Speaker 4) suggested applying for

Meeting Summary:

Mario met with Nancy to discuss the classification of his company, Drone Pilot Academy (DPA), within the context of Ann Arbor SPARK's Business Accelerator Fund (BAF) program and to strategize next steps, particularly concerning a major opportunity with the US military.

  1. Classification Issue: Following a meeting with Sarah from SPARK, Mario learned that while Airbots is classified as "high tech," Drone Pilot Academy is currently considered only "innovative." To qualify for potentially uncapped BAF funding ("high growth"), DPA needs to be reclassified as "high tech" or "advanced tech." Mario emphasized DPA is a software company, not a service provider, a distinction Sarah seemed to accept.
  2. Case for Advanced Tech: Nancy reviewed the criteria for "advanced tech" (new, novel, IP-protected innovations in specific sectors). Bot

Summary/Action: I met the client by zoom to discuss their technology and how it might fit for SBIR/STTR. We discussed the SBIR/STTR programs in general: Brief History of the SBIR Program, SBIR Program Objectives, SBIR Eligibility, SBIR Three Phases, Brief History of the STTR Program, STTR Program Objectives, STTR Small Business Eligibility, STTR Non-Profit Institution Eligibility, STTR Three Phases, Advantages of SBIR/STTR Programs, Three Types of SBIR Projects, Contract Agencies, Grant Agencies, Research Topics and Agency Solicitations, Phase I success, Phase II success, Possible SBIR Timeline, Is SBIR/STTR right for the company? We went over the details of the program and how I can work with them. I told them that I am a high-energy physicist who had a small business in Virginia before coming to Michigan. The R&D of our particle accelerator research company was funded by the SBIR program and we had over 30 awards (phase I and II) in 8 of the federal agencies before selling off

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Reviewed Lisa's initial financials (basic inc/exp) in detail. Income projections are too aggressive and unrealistic. Client based her income numbers on her production capacity. She will have another person working with her in her food truck and she has 8 years of experience in fast food and understands the production capacity easily. She'll now work on determining capacity WITH the realistic sales of her products as a brand new business. We'll need a few sessions together to determine those numbers. We reviewed expenses in detail and talked through contract vs employee labor compensation. Client would like to be able to begin operating her truck this Summer and is prioritizing getting the numbers right first. Next steps - client updating numbers and written plan and will schedule another session in 10+ days to register her LLC!

I met with our client by zoom call. We explored whether she would be able to obtain an EIN for her businesses she formed. The three entities share her as the common, 100% owner of each company. She is a Canadian citizen and does not have a green card. She will contact the Detroit IRS office (easier for her to access from Canada) to see how she would obtain the EIN numbers.


We then moved to discussion of her business plan. At issue is that some of the information does not line up with what is in the financials. We discussed why this had to be and where the discrepancies have occurred (largely by her use of AI to generate content). I will do some of the work tomorrow and we have a meeting set for 4:00p to review progress on the plan.

Met Barb at the SEDP. Mel wants more decision making responsibilities. Don is there every day and has tried to implement tip sharing which is against Barrister Brewing Co policies. Don already gave a cook a raise and now he wants to give him more money.


Daytime Hours - Daytime is not busy. Barb would like to close 11am-3pm during the week and open Tuesday and Wednesday Nights.


They implemented Craft Night on Thursday Nights. November - 19 people showed up and it was marketed 3-4 weeks in advance. December - 9-10 people showed up. January the event was only posted 1 week ahead. March posted 3 days before and no one showed up.


Trivia Night on Monday has been a steady evening now and it's worth being open. They have on average 7 teams. She and Dan created the entire program.


Issue: Consistency in marketing and class scheduling.


Christie has access to make Barb and Admin on FB and also

She needs these documents to calm the Fenton City officials about their status and get noticed so that other residents can participate in events and plantings. She has focused on this effort for 2 years and was getting close to quitting it. She thanked me for helping her. I told her to expect the LARA form via email in 3-4 days.

Brad and I spoke on the phone this evening. He has an interest in developing an organic skin care products line, and to start this with an online platform. He indicated that he has a label and box design complete. He states that he does have recipes that he has gotten off the Internet for the products he is making.


He had to cut the meeting short. We will reschedule for tomorrow at 10 AM.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Analysis: After several missed appointments, we finally had the opportunity to meet. She has shifted her focus from the unicorn-themed business idea to selling trendy t-shirts with various sayings. The new direction is aimed at appealing to a younger audience.

Recommendation: To ensure her t-shirts resonate with her target audience, she needs to clearly define who that audience is and tailor her marketing accordingly. Additionally, it’s crucial for her to revamp her social media presence, selecting colors and refining her brand personality to align with her new product line. This will help create a cohesive brand image that connects with potential customers.

Next Steps:

  1. Complete the brand organizer to clarify her brand’s core identity and target audience.
  2. Complete the personality exercise to define her brand’s tone and style, ensuring consistency across all platforms and materials.

Summary: The DoT sent the client an update amendment to the solicitation that they just applied for. She is worried that this affects their funding and wanted to know what it meant.


Action: I looked over the document and I responded that I suspect that this is all cost-cutting. All three of these changes outlined will save them program administrative money which I am guessing they have been tasked to do. They will no longer have a pitch day meaning that the client will either get the award based on this application or they won’t. Previously they have offered a “pitch day” to what I suspect were their bubble awardees – those that were too close in ranking to decide between. They will not do that this year. Further, they formerly offered an opportunity to respond to their review comments and try to change their minds based on a rebuttal document. They were the only agency that allowed this and they will no longer do so. Finally, they will not contract with a business

CRITICAL PROBLEM: Potential termination of grant cohort funds due to inactivity. Reviewed case files to see what he has done to date, and its very little; requested by HUB to weed out the ones not active so they can reallocate the funds to active clients.


Hi Justin, 


I know when you signed up for the cohort, you were hoping that the grant funds would help you get employees onboarded and help with their first few months of costs. Since you haven't been active with the cohort, they are giving our 2024 grant winners until May to use their funds or you will be terminated from the program. The allowable uses for funds are still the same: 

  1. Legal assistance with contracts or other legal matters, and we are looking for a less expensive local attorney to help with that. 
  2. Marketing service assistance: Website design, marketing materials, video production, imprinted promotional products, lead generation

Lengthy discussion about the tariffs and how they will affect her business. Decision made to show tariff costs as a separate line item on her invoices to the customers rather than roll the cost into the price. This makes particular sense because the tariff level is fluid. they may be negotiated down. SenseComp lost money in January and February but made a $45,000 profit in March which puts the YTD as slightly profitable. The test center has had a very poor YTD with only 1 test for $15,000 so far this year. This operation is costing her about $25,000 per month with the building costs. Margaret's goal is to work her way down to about 20 hours per week. She plans to make Hahim the head of the operation. He had an employment offer, but Margaret talked him into staying. Margaret's CFO is leaving but will continue to do Margaret's books part-time.

Hi Jasmine,

Thanks for taking the time to meet so I could clarify my thoughts.


I've updated your beautiful business plan to simply mention that once we finalize the larger 36-month forecast, we'll want to make sure that those numbers match what you declare on page 7 (slide 12) of the written plan. (Note is attached to indicate the same on that slide)


On the CBJ 36-month forecast spreadsheet, please fill in the last tab "Sources and Use of Funds" to include where you will seek funding/investment and then how you will apply that investment across the project. The two totals must match.  


Otherwise, we'll continue to discuss the sources of funds and update the 36-month forecast to reflect the most accurate information we have. Both of these tools are used when you apply for both the grants and the loans.


Please reach out with any additional questions and feel free to book a session anytime i

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Meeting with Brian from SIMPL about issues with dev team, development velocity, and current large refactoring project. Talked through issues with refactor of current app taking 6-8 months so far and having another estimated 6 months to go. Development velocity has slowed considerably, and it’s difficult to determine appropriate next steps or how to remedy. Advised on helpful process for framing discussions with developers (first agreeing on ideal outcome, then separately discussing how best to get there and what compromises must be made for the business, etc.). Set up follow-up meeting for Monday.

Summary/Action: I met the client by zoom to discuss their technology and how it might fit for SBIR/STTR. We discussed the SBIR/STTR programs in general: Brief History of the SBIR Program, SBIR Program Objectives, SBIR Eligibility, SBIR Three Phases, Brief History of the STTR Program, STTR Program Objectives, STTR Small Business Eligibility, STTR Non-Profit Institution Eligibility, STTR Three Phases, Advantages of SBIR/STTR Programs, Three Types of SBIR Projects, Contract Agencies, Grant Agencies, Research Topics and Agency Solicitations, Phase I success, Phase II success, Possible SBIR Timeline, Is SBIR/STTR right for the company? We went over the details of the program and how I can work with them. I told them that I am a high-energy physicist who had a small business in Virginia before coming to Michigan. The R&D of our particle accelerator research company was funded by the SBIR program and we had over 30 awards (phase I and II) in 8 of the federal agencies before selling off

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Revenue this year should be about $12,000,0000. Pete's effort to sell the business with the assistance of Generational Equity. He has 1 interested party currently: Gloo, a company which is church focused. They made a verbal offer for an asset purchase of $500,000 cash and $500,000 stock in Gloo. Pete feels that he can talk them into a much larger amount because they need to show their investors revenue growth. Pete's company currently has a negative net worth and about $3.5 million of debt to pay off. He meets with the potential purchasers tomorrow.

Jeremy has hired a full-time integrator who he is paying $176,000. The company had a good quarter with a net profit of $250,000. Business is good; he needs to hire a full-time outside sales person. He is also looking for tech people. Cash is tight. His Account Receivable are very high with over $600,000 over 90 days. CMMC (cyber security compliance for his US Government contracts) is costing him over $225,000.

Kelly is having problems with his 3 sales staff. They are not bringing in much new business. He may have to fire them all. In other matters, one of the businesses he inherited from his father has a tenant that is not paying rent. He has had to engage an attorney in Georgia to settle the matter.

I met with Brandie Ivy yesterday regarding her Turo car rental business she plans on starting. Per our conversation, I recommended the following:

  1. Update the Start-Up Cost Summary to reflect costs for one year, including a business license from the city you will be operating from, parking fees, etc. 
  2. Under Product Description in the Business Plan, add service add ons , i.e. roadside assistance.
  3. In the Business Plan, identify the Market size and segments, describe your target market i.e. who are your best potential customers (Section IIC), your Competition IID (see attached) and your Marketing & Sales Plan (Section IIE). 
  4. Open a business bank account that's convenient to you and talk to the business banker about small business support tools, i.e. line of credit, business credit card or you can discuss those possibilities with Drusilla Fanning. Attached is what she will require for the LIFT Loan program. 

Good luck on your meeting

Set appointment


Thank you for your responses. Please use https://calendly.com/southeastmisbdc/phone-call to set a meeting. 


What to Expect

  • We are funded by the Small Business Administration and our services are at no-cost (except some trainings have a small registration fee). 
  • All information we discuss will remain confidential. This information was in the initial documentation that you completed when you requested consulting.
  • Meetings usually last less than 1 hour. Meetings are only by phone and by appointment.
  • We assist both start-up and existing businesses. Some of the topics that we help with are startup steps, business plans, marketing strategies, financial benchmarking, projections and preparing for bank loans.
  • Michigan SBDC does not have any direc

I met with Becky to review her original Executive Summary for the Great Lakes Bay Business Hub Grant. We reviewed the document on its own, as well as in conjunction with feedback from the reviewers. The feedback was consistent across all three score cards, so we felt confident that these were the areas of focus. There are some areas where Raby's cannot change scores - for example, applicants are penalized for having fewer employees. However, we found areas to improve in the following ways:

  • Note the marked growth in revenue since November 2024 - up from ~$7,000 to ~$29,000
  • Discussion of the specific marketing strategies that have been put into place: Facebook, radio, flyering, and community events
  • Discussion of Becky's involvement in the community - she has been supporting other organizations' events, and networking when possible.
  • Update the sales strategy and financials to discuss league pricing, private events, etc. as additional reven

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Hi Caroline, 


I know you will be revising the payroll numbers so I won't comment on those now. I think you have more products; 1/2 day am, 1/2 day pm, 1/2 day am drop in and 1/2 day drop in pm. The rate for am should be higher than the rate for pm, because it will be the most sought after. You should even go further and offer M/W/F and T/Th schedules and pricing along with 5 days a week for the am and pm times. I think closing for lunch is best and having the parents provide all snacks and beverages other than water. You don't want hot beverages in any area children are passing through. You can require parents that bring a hot beverage must keep it in a spill proof container at the workstation.

Consider starting August with 25% of capacity. (5 kids/parents 3 days a week). You can increase by 10% enrollment per month. Take the line item for flowers off, if you choose to purchase a plant or flowers then it is part of supplies. The additional labor for po

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Prep: Review financial projections.

Analysis: She has completed her financial projections, and we reviewed them together. During our session, she made adjustments to her financial plan and demonstrated a clear understanding of the changes. However, she was missing some personal finance documentation, which we incorporated into her plan.

Recommendation: While waiting to submit her financials, it would be beneficial for her to start focusing on building her social media presence. Since she plans to open her business in May of this year, establishing an online presence now will allow her to generate interest and build an audience ahead of the launch.

Next Steps:

  1. Begin working on her social media strategy to create content and engage with potential customers.
  2. Attend networking events to expand her professional connections and increase her visibility within her industry.

Quick recap 


Matt and Nancy discussed their ongoing colds and the transition of tech documentation responsibilities to interns, with a focus on startup valuations and the potential for repurposing the Wonder Glade platform for a parking app. They also discussed the pricing of their business acquisition, with Matt expressing concerns about the initial valuation and the increasing losses. The conversation ended with Matt discussing his commitment to investing in the company, hiring interns, and plans for the future of the project, including the implementation of an automated test suite.

Janet is working on amending her taxes. She is working with a retired CPA who is her sons Mother-in-law. Her monthly cash flow still did not match her Schedule C. We discussed the areas of question, which she will check with her CPA.

  1. $14,300 Credit Card payments, what can be expensed the whole amount or the interest of $4,929?
  2. Why is her COGS number $39,074 but her monthly cash flow line 24, column N show $51,912? She stated she does not pay her consignment unless she makes the sale, which should be a COGS. The $500 for Snow removal should be a fixed expense.
  3. She is showing $28,917 in Net Profit but according to the Cash Flow spreadsheet if she adds her Owner Investment of $8,986 plus $14,300 credit card, minus $4,929 credit card interest, plus $5,319 Cash Reserve (envelope money) plus ending cash balance of $1,245 this totals $24,911.
  4. Updated her discounts and allowances to show cash sales of $125,044.

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

I reviewed the Dawning Lace Doula Services website and provided feedback on how to improve its structure, functionality, and ability to convert visitors into clients. The client asked for strategic suggestions specific to website improvement. I identified several areas of concern, including a lack of mobile optimization, no clear call-to-actions (CTAs), limited navigation, and the absence of a contact form or booking system. I also noted missed opportunities for local SEO and social proof, both of which are important for service-based businesses like doula work.


RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

I recommended that the client add anchor links or a sticky men

Analysis: She has decided to pause her business operations to focus on rebranding. As part of this rebrand, she plans to change her business name and introduce three new components under it. While she will maintain the same color scheme, she intends to refresh her website and update her social media platforms accordingly.

Recommendation: To ensure her rebrand is successful, she must first define her target audience, which will help tailor her messaging and marketing efforts. Additionally, preparing a speaker sheet will be valuable as it can help position her as an authority in her field and support her marketing efforts. This will also help solidify her brand’s credibility.

Next Steps:

  1. Complete the brand organizer to clarify her brand identity and ensure the rebrand aligns with her vision.
  2. Take a personality trait to better understand her brand’s tone and style, which will guide her messaging.

**Should capture impact of start up** And assist with development of marketing/record keeping.

Lance Wolfe <lwolfe@mtu.edu>

Introductory client meeting. Two businesses: Alexander and Phillips - consulting services and MyCampusKorner - web based student services platform. Looking for feedback on his business plans and looking for how to attract consulting clients

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

Kristi put a sign on the building and they've received a lot of interest including a letter of intent. The couple wants the space to turn it into a chiropractic/wellness center.


The problem is due to her current financing, the only way they could do a seller finance situation and transfer everyrthing over is if they want to continue to run it as a brewput. They want to run it as is for the summer, close for the winter and then start renovating. The problem would be transfering the liquor license will take longer than they anticipate.





RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

She tell the seller the situation and go through traditional bank financing.




NEXT STEPS & FOLLOW-UP ACTIONS:

she is going to talk with her attorney and lender





IMPACT REMINDER: client is aware

Nick's numbers do not support being able to buy a building. My suggestion is that he rent space first and then prove his ability to deliver on the numbers prior to buying a property. He has never run a fitness facility and does not have a degree or certification in personal training or fitness. This may be a big lift.

email to Nick today:

Thanks for the information Nick. It's a good first start. 

There is no harm in doing the research and I suggest we get the financial details in place before pursuing properties. We'll want to establish what's within your budget.

The larger Financial Model is now missing the potential interest rate of your loan, which helps to envision what may be possible. We'll work on this on our end and return it to you. 

On April 29th, we can discuss what is possible, based on arriving at more solid numbers.


With Gratitude,

Wendy

D'Myron originally wanted to create a community of creators and generate some passive income. We ultimately got to D'Myron making updates to his website to have an "About Us" page - he has no story about himself and how he got to his present status with many successful projects. He will also begin a blog - writing 3 to 4 posts prior to reaching out to his community of creators to invite them, too, to post on his page (have links back to their site). He will also begin building a strategy around outsourcing other creatives for his projects (photographers, videographers, visual artists) and have them as subcontractors to his contracts with clients. Example: create a contract for them that allows him to charge a fee to market and manage them to his clients. (10 - 20% markup?) I am also sending the Basic Inc.Exp to get started on his financial planning exercise. (SCORE is way too complicated at the moment). Next meeting is set for 4/29.

Re: Spreadsheet

Camer

The client's are in a dire financial situation. I received a phone call from their loan officer Derek Edwards to help them put together a letter of explaination that gives Invest Detroit details of where the company is and where the company is heading.

The reason being is that J & G Pallets owes over $175,000 outstanding water bills, property taxes, and commercial property insurance, in other words they are at severe risk where they can loose the property.


Derek is trying to get a full assessment of the situation so that he can determine how to move forward. It's important to note that the owners have accepted a LOI interest to sell the property for 2.5 million dollars which would allow them to payoff debts to Invest Detroit as well as the outstanding water bill, property taxes, and to bring their insurance in good standing. The value of the property is TBD because there are repairs and the potential owner wants the property to be in a HUB zone.

Hi Tyler,

Home Depot does offer pro and commercial credit cards through Citibank. Both will require a personal guarantee since your bu

I met with Barima Opong-Owusu from Ankra Health on April 14, 2025, and started by introducing the SBDC tech team, highlighting our core competencies and how we support tech-based startups. I shared our team's expertise in areas such as SBIR grants, legal review, patent landscaping, financial modeling, and digital health strategy.

Barima shared his background as a clinical pharmacist and his inspiration for founding Ankra Health. His startup focuses on generating a personalized “health score” using lab results and applying AI to deliver actionable health and wellness recommendations.

He mentioned needing legal guidance, especially around IP protection and formalizing his partnership with a software developer. I walked him through potential funding sources, including the SBIR grant program and the Business Accelerator Fund (BAF) through Michigan’s SmartZones, which could support prototype development.

Barima also shared insights from early focus group research su

Spoke with Naomi, she was on my aging clients list so I gave her a call. She did not move forward with this opportunity after reviewing the financials more in dept. She is the general manager now of a chicken restaurant in Clio - 11/11 Crispy Chicken & Burgers. She said they have had a lot of staff turnover and general manager turnover in the past year so it is like she is starting something from scratch. She said she is running the business without it being her own. I told her what a great opportunity. Have someone pay you to run their business, gain the knowledge and educate yourself while you're being paid so when you're ready to explore entrepreneurship again you will have gained so much during this time. I told her she can reach out the the SBDC in the future, when she decides on another endeavor she may want to explore.


For now this case can be closed, client is welcome to re-engage in the future and was informed as such.

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM: 

Valarie came into Berston needing assistance with her LLC. She had received an email from LARA but was not sure what they needed from her after we filed her annual statements last month. I gave her the LARA phone number and she called. LARA said she needed to file the Certificate of Good Standing before she could be reinstated. We got her CID and PIN and were able to log in. I helped her file the certificate and she paid the $50 fee. 


RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

• Hired someone to do branding design
  • Cannot find products at a reasonable price, to also sell at a reasonable price. Struggling to justify investing in inventory now, especially without a store front
  • We talked about what to do to pivot - we agreed to start to grow the brand and following on social media, to later launch products slowly
  • I will send her some links to videos that she may want to replicate/pull inspo from
  • Her homework is to start getting comfortable with recording and taking content

Discussion on progress and sale timeline. Wants to sell in 2027 Spring. Why? Discussed primary motivations/desires from sale: Mission-driven, $ Value, Timeline of 2 years due to stress. Challenged him on the why, what could make his business so appealing that he wouldn't want to sell it. Discussed how sales typically work. He thinks a landscaping company could be a good targeted buyer. Floated the idea of accountant buyer, hiring key employee to become a buyer, changing his business so that he can step away from daily work while still meeting his mission and money generation goals. Sent him Guide to Selling for reading for next appointment.

ClientIntake.CompanyName: Radford LLC DBA Willow and Co. Spa

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Met with Crystal via telephone. She has been dealing with a client of LaBella who purchased a gift certificate for her mother in the amount of $70.00. Crystal purchased the business for $70,000 from Danielle. Come to find out Danielle purchased the business for $50,000 2 years before selling and didn't upgrade any of the equipment she acquired in the purchase nor was maintenance done on the equipment. Crystal has received a loan for $50,000 through Michigan Women Forward and has put in new pedicure chairs, nail stations and hair stations for the booth renters that operate in the space.


She wasn't given a credit on the purchase of the business from LaBella for the gift cards they had sold. The owner took the money for those as well as the monthly payment Crystal has been making towards the purchase of the business. Crystal has been in touch with a Lawyer because this has now gone to FB and screenshots from Danielle have been posted.

I met with Tom and Jeanine, along with Elizabeth Propp of CMURC to discuss the Great Lakes Bay Business Hub. Elizabeth gave the couple an overview of the Hub resources, as well as walked through the grant application pieces, as well as allowable use of funds.

Tom gave us some background on the business, and praised Jeff Punches for all of his assistance since the start up phase. After hearing more about his operation, Elizabeth mentioned connecting him with the Blue Angels Investment group, as there is an event coming up tomorrow. Hi Tom,

 

It was a pleasure speaking with you and learning more about your business.
